_meta.comment: PLACEHOLDER baseline for #0000 A/B matcher-quality gate. Capturing the true live baseline requires launching the Unreal Editor, triggering a tool-catalog rebuild against the hybrid FTS5+semantic matcher (mcp_index_engine + mcp_tool_catalog), and dumping top-K results per query. That workflow is not available in this non-interactive environment, so this file ships as a documented placeholder. Per the sequencing note in WORKFLOW_STATE.json, the live capture must be performed by an operator BEFORE S4 deletes mcp_index_engine.py. This placeholder lets S5 verify the fixture schema and compare-driver wiring; the PR description must flag that the real baseline capture is pending operator action.
capture_procedure: 1. Check out this branch (llm/<user>/<workspace>/<task>) at HEAD of S3 implementation (pre-S4).
2. Launch UnrealEditor with the Claireon plugin loaded.
3. Open the Claireon REPL / diagnostics widget and ensure the tool catalog is rebuilt (trigger a claireon.tools_search once to warm the catalog).
4. For each non-blank, non-comment line in tool_catalog_ab_queries.txt, execute Python in the REPL:
5. Record {query: [top-5 tool_name strings in rank order]} into this file's 'results' object, replacing the placeholder entries below.
6. Commit the populated file under this path with subject 'impl(#0000): S3 - capture live A/B baseline'.
7. S5 will then run the same fixture against the new C++ matcher, emit tool_catalog_candidate.json, and compute average top-5 intersection per test-plan.md section 5.
_meta.matcher_under_capture: hybrid FTS5 keyword + semantic (RRF fuse) via mcp_index_engine.get_engine()
